description: During a scooping run on local water source, a civilian pleasure craft entered the step-path zone from a hidden cove. Operator of the watercraft appeared unaware of active air tanker operations despite local NOTAMs. Left-hand turn initiated early to terminate scoop safely and clear watercraft.
update: This report has been reviewed with Flight Operations and closed with no further investigation required. The Fleet Manager will conduct a debrief with the pilot and the information submitted will be used for trend analysis.

description: Substantial migratory bird activity observed near the approach path for Runway 25 during high-frequency retardant reloading cycles. Flocks of Canada geese nesting in the slough just off the airport boundary are increasingly active during peak afternoon heat.
update: Grande Prairie Airport authority contacted. Bird sweeps scheduled during active tanker reload windows. Operations monitoring.

description: Intermittent telemetry dropouts on the gel injection system computer during drop runs. System reverted to manual retardant gate controls successfully. No drop pattern distortion noted, but telemetry was lost for the duration of the drop.
update: Initial inspection underway. Wiring harness behind the control console to be checked for pin wear or loose ground terminal.

description: Retardant loading hose couplers at the reloading pits are showing signs of heavy rubber degradation at the stress joints, creating a potential high-pressure blowout hazard during rapid reload hot-loading sequences.
update: All reload pit coupler hoses inspected. Two worn hoses swapped out for fresh assemblies. Preventative maintenance check added to weekly checklist.
